title: Carlomohria parviflora
intro: Carlomohria parviflora, also known as Small-flowered Carlomohria, is a plant species in the family Boraginaceae, characterized by its small flowers and unique leaf structure. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and oxidative stress. Bioactive constituents of Carlomohria parviflora include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used in herbal medicine, with the most common preparations including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential side effects.
